description: Repro of a concurrency incident: a read-modify-write on a shared counter is not atomic, so concurrent requests lose updates. The concurrency-async lens must catch it and block.

evidence_trace: src/services/quota.ts:34 reads `count`, adds 1 in JS, then writes it back
the handler runs per-request with no lock or atomic increment
two concurrent requests read the same value and one increment is lost
finding.model_authored_prose: This read-modify-write on `count` is not atomic — concurrent requests will lose increments. Use an atomic DB increment (`UPDATE ... SET count = count + 1`) instead.
